summary: High‑energy second set with monster The Other One and scorching Scarlet>Fire
blurb: The concert launches with a solid first set that builds into a blistering second set, highlighted by a monster The Other One and a scorching Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain. Phil’s thunderous intro on The Other One and tight drum work keep the momentum rolling, while Jack Straw opens the night sweetly.
review: On August 16, 1981 at McArthur Court in Eugene, the Dead turned up the heat despite a sweltering arena. The first set kept things tight, kicking off with a bright‑sounding Jack Straw and cruising through classics like Deal and Little Red Rooster. It was the second set that truly ignited the crowd: Phil’s booming "buh‑buh‑Buh‑BUH‑BOOM" intro launched a monster rendition of The Other One, which segued into an inspired Stella Blue before the band dove into one of the night’s most celebrated passages – Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain, followed by an expansive Estimated Prophet into Eyes Of The World. Drums and percussion were especially tight, driving the transitions with a joyous, high‑energy vibe that reviewers repeatedly called “groovy” and “joyous”. Though some tape hiss is present on certain sources, the overall sound captures the band’s relentless flow and makes this show a must‑listen for fans of the early‑’80s era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings (2 positive, 2 mixed) consistently praise the show’s high energy, monster The Other One, scorching Scarlet Begonias > Fire sequence, and tight drums, with no notable negative commentary, warranting a moderate positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Solid‑sounding source with clear audio, no dropouts, and captures the monster The Other One and scorching Scarlet>Fire highlighted by reviewers.

key_highlights: Monster performance of The Other One in the second set.
Scorching Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain sequence.
Tight, awesome drumming driving the high‑energy flow.

band_performance.Jerry: While no specific solos are singled out, Jerry’s guitar work rides the high‑energy vibe throughout, contributing to the overall joy and intensity of the performance.
band_performance.Phil: Delivers a thunderous "buh‑buh‑Buh‑BUH‑BOOM" intro on The Other One that fans still recall years later.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums are described as tight and awesome, especially the second‑set opener leading into The Other One.
band_performance.Billy: Alongside Mickey, provides a solid, driving rhythm that keeps the momentum fierce during the extended jams.
